title: Sales Department
learning_objectives: Understand the role of the Sales Department.
Learn the sales process in a manufacturing organization.
Recognize coordination between Sales and other departments.
Understand the use of SAP SD in sales operations.

introduction.title: What is the Sales Department?
introduction.content: The Sales Department is responsible for understanding customer requirements, generating business opportunities, processing customer orders and ensuring products are delivered according to agreed specifications and timelines. It serves as the primary communication channel between customers and the organization.

business_purpose.title: Why is the Sales Department Important?
business_purpose.content: The Sales Department generates revenue by securing customer orders and maintaining strong customer relationships. It communicates market demand to internal departments, helping the organization plan production, inventory and delivery efficiently.

sales_workflow: Create Sales Order in SAP SD.

key_responsibilities.description: Develop and maintain long-term relationships with customers.
Create, monitor and manage customer orders.
Prepare commercial quotations based on customer requirements.
Monitor customer demand and market trends.
Estimate future sales to support production planning.
Handle customer inquiries, complaints and after-sales communication.

daily_activities: Coordinate with Production for delivery schedules.

key_sales_concepts.description: Managing customer interactions to improve satisfaction and retention.
Predicting future customer demand to support planning.
Completing customer orders accurately and on time.
Providing assistance after product delivery to ensure customer satisfaction.

coordination_with_departments.purpose: Plans manufacturing based on confirmed customer orders.
Addresses customer quality concerns and product specifications.
Processes customer orders, deliveries and billing.

business_example.title: Sales Operations at JSL
business_example.content: A customer places an order for stainless steel coils with specific dimensions and delivery requirements. The Sales Department creates a Sales Order in SAP SD and coordinates with Production to schedule manufacturing. Warehouse confirms finished goods availability, Dispatch arranges transportation and Finance generates the customer invoice. After delivery, Sales collects customer feedback to strengthen future business relationships.

common_errors: Failure to follow up after delivery.
important_notes: Customer satisfaction is a shared responsibility across all departments.
Sales forecasts help optimize production planning.
Long-term customer relationships are built through reliability and service quality.
